methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $174M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Oxa
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Zeitview is a Series D-stage autonomous drone company founded by Daniel Burton and Eli Tamanaha. The company operates within the Drones & Autonomous sector, where a peer set exists comprising five private companies—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Oxa—but no direct public comparables. Zeitview has raised $174M to date across its funding history.

Valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression method, which proves applicable given Zeitview's clear Series D positioning. This approach applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D-to-later-stage advancement to the $174M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $261M. A peer-median approach could not be applied; although four same-sector peers exist, none satisfied narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables—requiring a minimum of three datapoints for statistical validity. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The analysis triangulates to a valuation range of $222M (low) to $300M (high), with a median of $261M. This range reflects a single applicable method and rests on the assumption that typical Series D step-ups hold for this company. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow peer benchmarking data and the reliance on a raised-capital proxy rather than explicit prior-round valuations. Stage-appropriate revenue or profitability multiples remain unavailable. Additional due diligence on recent comparable transactions and the target's current burn rate would strengthen conviction in this range.
